source: Exact oracle values dumped from the REAL RMC.BestFit MaximumLikelihood via tools/oracle_emitter --dump (M14) and reproduced by tools/verify_oracles.py. Exercises the `competing_risks` model type: component families through the factory into CompetingRisksModel -- which is NOT an IUnivariateModel (the C# omits it) but IS a ModelBase, which is all the estimators require. Parameter layout: the concatenated component parameters [Gumbel location, Gumbel scale, Weibull scale, Weibull shape]. The M13 smoke dataset pinned the Weibull shape at ~99.998 (its 100 constraint bound); this dataset (25 draws of max(Gumbel, Weibull) processes, seed-derived, hand-rounded) fits fully interior (shape ~13.02). NelderMead point values rel 1e-9; standard errors rel 5e-3, a DOCUMENTED looser tier than the Phase 4 1e-5 Hessian band: for O(1e4)-scale parameters NumericalDiff clamps the step to MaxStep = 1e-2, so the Hessian second differences sit close to the double-rounding cancellation floor -- measured C++-vs-C# divergence up to ~1.1e-3 (pure noise; the fit itself matches to ~1e-12). T14 (BestFit v2.0.0, commit 1abe795 'Guard composite Jeffreys scale priors') is NOT expressible declaratively in this file: the single-parameter-family Jeffreys-guard scenario needs a component distribution (e.g. Poisson) outside the model's own 15-family whitelist, and the shared spec builder's CompetingRisksModel(DataFrame, families) construction path always runs SetDefaultParameters -> dynamic_cast<IMaximumLikelihoodEstimation&> on every component, which throws std::bad_cast for such a component before a fixture case could ever reach the prior-likelihood surface being guarded. Covered by C++-only ctest instead (the GMM sticky-fallback precedent in this file's neighbor): core/tests/test_competing_risks_model.cpp's test_jeffreys_prior_one_parameter_component_omits_scale_contribution / test_jeffreys_prior_mixed_components_applies_only_available_scale_contribution / test_jeffreys_prior_nonpositive_scale_returns_negative_infinity.
